requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for fetching delivery records and milk production records by month/year
acceptanceCriteria: A hook exists for fetching delivery records filtered by month and year
A hook exists for fetching milk production records filtered by month and year
Hooks use the existing actor pattern from useQueries.ts

file_operations.description: Add two new query hooks: useGetDeliveryRecordsByMonth(month: number, year: number) and useGetMilkRecordsByMonth(month: number, year: number). Both hooks should call the corresponding backend methods (getDeliveryRecordsByMonth and getMilkRecordsByMonth) using the actor pattern established in this file. Use React Query's useQuery with query keys that include month and year parameters for proper cache invalidation. Enable the queries only when the actor is available.

requirements.summary: Create Monthly Reports page with month/year selector at route /reports
acceptanceCriteria: Route /reports renders the Monthly Reports page
Page has a month and year selector defaulting to the current month/year
Changing the month/year selector refreshes all report data on the page
Page is wrapped in the existing Layout component and uses the farm-themed card-based design

file_operations.description: Create a new page component that renders the Monthly Reports interface. Include state management for selected month and year (defaulting to current month/year using new Date()). Render a month/year selector at the top using select elements styled consistently with the farm theme. Include two main sections below the selector: Monthly Delivery Report and Monthly Milk Production Report. Wrap the entire page in a Layout component and use the card-based design pattern with warm greens and ambers matching the existing pages (bg-surface, border-primary classes). Pass the selected month and year to child components or hooks that fetch and display the data.

Add a new route for /reports that renders the MonthlyReports page component. Import the MonthlyReports component and add it to the router configuration alongside the existing routes (/, /cattle, /milk, /inventory, /customers, /deliveries). Ensure the route is wrapped in the Layout component consistent with other routes.

requirements.summary: Display Monthly Delivery Report section with summary totals, per-customer breakdown, and per-delivery-boy performance tables
acceptanceCriteria: Summary totals card shows correct aggregated counts for the selected month
Per-customer breakdown table shows one row per customer with their delivery stats
Per-delivery-boy table shows one row per delivery boy with their performance stats
All data reflects the selected month and year

file_operations.description: In the Monthly Delivery Report section, call useGetDeliveryRecordsByMonth with the selected month and year. Calculate summary totals: total deliveries count (all records), total missed deliveries count (status === 'missed'), and total liters delivered (sum of quantityLiters for delivered records). Display these in a summary card at the top of the section. Below the summary, create two tables: (1) Per-customer breakdown: group delivery records by customerId, fetch customer names using the existing useGetCustomers hook, and display each customer's name, total deliveries received (count of records for that customer), total missed (count where status === 'missed'), and total liters (sum of quantityLiters). (2) Per-delivery-boy performance: group delivery records by deliveryBoyName and display each delivery boy's name, total deliveries made (count of records), total missed (count where status === 'missed'), and total liters delivered (sum of quantityLiters). Style tables consistently with the existing table patterns in DeliveryReports.tsx and CustomerManagement.tsx using border-primary and hover states.

requirements.summary: Display Monthly Milk Production Report section with summary card and per-cattle breakdown table
acceptanceCriteria: Summary card shows total milk production in liters for the selected month
Per-cattle breakdown table shows one row per cattle with total liters for the month
Data updates when the month/year selector changes

file_operations.description: In the Monthly Milk Production Report section, call useGetMilkRecordsByMonth with the selected month and year. Calculate the total liters produced by summing the quantityLiters field of all returned records. Display this total in a summary card. Note: The current MilkProductionRecord type does not include a cattleId field, so the per-cattle breakdown cannot be implemented with the existing backend schema. Display a note in the UI stating 'Per-cattle breakdown requires cattleId field in milk production records' or display the records without cattle association. If cattleId becomes available in the future, group records by cattleId and display each cattle's name/ID with their total liters produced. Use table styling consistent with other report tables.

requirements.summary: Add print-friendly view with 'Print Report' button and print-specific CSS
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Print Report' button is visible on the page
Clicking the button triggers the browser print dialog
When printing, navigation bar and interactive controls are hidden
Report tables and summary cards are fully visible and legible in the printed output

file_operations.description: Add a 'Print Report' button near the top of the page (after the month/year selector) that calls window.print() when clicked. Style the button with the existing farm theme using bg-primary and hover:bg-primary/90 classes. Add a className 'print:hidden' to the button, the month/year selector, and any other interactive controls that should not appear in the printed version. Ensure the Layout component's navigation bar is hidden when printing (the Layout component should already handle this or add print:hidden to navigation elements if needed). Verify that report content (summary cards and tables) uses print-friendly styles with clear borders and black text on white background.

Add print-specific CSS media query rules at the end of the file. Hide elements with .print\:hidden class when printing. Ensure report tables and cards have visible borders and legible text in print mode. Set background colors to white and text colors to black for printed content. Remove any shadows or gradients that don't print well. Ensure page breaks are handled appropriately so tables don't split awkwardly across pages.

requirements.summary: Add 'Reports' navigation link to the Layout component pointing to /reports route
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reports' link appears in the desktop navigation bar
A 'Reports' link appears in the mobile hamburger menu
Clicking the link navigates to /reports
Active state styling matches the existing nav link pattern

file_operations.description: Add a new navigation link for 'Reports' pointing to the /reports route. Insert it in both the desktop navigation bar (alongside Dashboard, Cattle, Milk Production, Inventory, Customers, Deliveries) and the mobile hamburger menu. Use the same Link component pattern and active state styling (isActive check) as the existing nav links. Position the Reports link logically in the navigation order, perhaps after Deliveries since reports aggregate delivery and production data.
